lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: But are my brother’s powers set forth?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Ay, madam.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Himself in person there?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Madam, with much ado: Your sister is the better soldier.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Lord Edmund spake not with your lord at home?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: No, madam.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: What might import my sister’s letter to him?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I know not, lady.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: ’Faith, he is posted hence on serious matter.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: It was great ignorance, Gloucester’s eyes being out,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To let him live: where he arrives he moves

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: All hearts against us: Edmund, I think, is gone,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: In pity of his misery, to dispatch

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: His nighted life: moreover, to descry

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: The strength o’ the enemy.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I must needs after him, madam, with my letter.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Our troops set forth to-morrow: stay with us;

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: The ways are dangerous.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I may not, madam:

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: My lady charged my duty in this business.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Why should she write to Edmund? Might not you

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Transport her purposes by word? Belike,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Something—I know not what: I’ll love thee much,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Let me unseal the letter.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Madam, I had rather—

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I know your lady does not love her husband;

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I am sure of that: and at her late being here

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: She gave strange oeillades and most speaking looks

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: To noble Edmund. I know you are of her bosom.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: I, madam?

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I speak in understanding; you are; I know’t:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Therefore I do advise you, take this note:

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: My lord is dead; Edmund and I have talk’d;

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: And more convenient is he for my hand

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Than for your lady’s: you may gather more.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: If you do find him, pray you, give him this;

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: And when your mistress hears thus much from you,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: I pray, desire her call her wisdom to her.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: So, fare you well.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: If you do chance to hear of that blind traitor,

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Preferment falls on him that cuts him off.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Would I could meet him, madam! I should show

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: What party I do follow.

lines.speaker: REGAN
lines.text: Fare thee well.

annotations.title: Regan and Goneril's rivalry for Edmund — mutual destruction set in motion
annotations.body: With Cornwall dead, Regan is newly available and presses her claim to Edmund: 'I know your lady does not love her husband; / I am sure of that.' Goneril's letter, hidden on Oswald's body, will eventually surface. The rivalry is darkly funny — two women in their forties competing over a bastard half their age who is using them both — but it is also structurally decisive. Edmund's courtship of both sisters is the mechanism by which they will poison each other (Goneril poisoning Regan, then taking her own life). Shakespeare is letting Edmund be the knife each sister is handing the other. Their sexual rivalry is the play's most compressed study of how evil ultimately consumes itself — not because evil is self-punishing in general, but because Edmund is specifically more dangerous than anyone he plays.
sources: Marilyn French, Shakespeare's Division of Experience (1981)

trials.prompt: The sisters' rivalry for Edmund in 4.5 sets in motion —
options: Their mutual destruction — Edmund will be the knife each sister hands the other, ending in Goneril poisoning Regan and taking her own life
